'''Bailey Antrobus''' (born 18 February 2000) is a [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} and {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Antrobus was born in [[Wollongong]], [[New South Wales, Australia]].<ref name=YP311023/> He is of [[Wales|Welsh]] descent.<ref>[https://www.zerotackle.com/players/bailey-antrobus/ Zero Tackle profile]</ref>

==Playing career==
===York Knights===
Antrobus played for the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] in  trial games in [[2021 St. George Illawarra Dragons season|2021]]. On 13 May 2022, Antrobus signed a contract to join RFL Championship side [[York Knights]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/20138386.york-city-knights-sign-back-rower-bailey-antrobus/|title=York City Knights sign back-rower Bailey Antrobus|website=www.yorkpress.co.uk|date=13 May 2022 }}</ref> In August 2023, Antrobus suffered an [[Anterior cruciate ligament injury|ACL injury]] and missed the rest of the season. In October 2023, he signed a two-year deal to remain at York until 2025.<ref name=YP311023>{{cite news|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/23891896.york-knights-ace-bailey-antrobus-signs-new-two-year-contract/ |title=York Knights ace Bailey Antrobus signs new two-year contract |newspaper=The Press |date=31 October 2023 |access-date=29 February 2024}}</ref>

===Newcastle Thunder===
On 10 December 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=10 Dec 2025|author=Stephen Ibbetson|title=Newcastle Thunder sign York Knights forward as clubs boost partnership|url=https://www.totalrl.com/newcastle-thunder-sign-york-knights-forward-as-clubs-boost-partnership/}}</ref>

===International career===
He made his debut in June 2022 against {{rlnt|France}} at the [[Stadium Municipal d'Albi]] in [[Albi]].<ref>[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61025709 France 34-10 Wales: Rusty Wales given French lesson]</ref>
In 2022 Antrobus was named in the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup squads#Wales|Wales squad]] for the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup|2021 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/rugby-league-world-cup-2021-squad-lists/ Full list of every squad at the Rugby League World Cup 2021]</ref> Antrobus played in all three group stage games as Wales were eliminated after losing all their matches.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63464967|title=Rugby League World Cup: Papua New Guinea 36-0 Wales - Kumuls seal quarter-final against England|work=BBC Sport |date=31 October 2022 }}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/23092615.york-knights-ace-stars-wales-world-cup-defeat-png/|title=York Knights ace stars in Wales' World Cup defeat to PNG|newspaper=The Press|date=|access-date=1 November 2022}}</ref>
He played and co captained [[Wales]] against [[Jamaica]] in the second row coming up with a 24-16 win at home in [[Neath]].
